ProjectDescriptions.description: A list of project descriptions. The list is sorted by the date and time the projects are created.

NextToken.description: If the previous response was incomplete (because there is more results to retrieve), Amazon Rekognition returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of results.
If the result is truncated, the response provides a NextToken that you can use in the subsequent request to fetch the next set of collection IDs.

FaceModelVersions.description: Version numbers of the face detection models associated with the collections in the array CollectionIds. For example, the value of FaceModelVersions[2] is the version number for the face detection model used by the collection in CollectionId[2].

NextToken.description: Pagination token, if the previous response was incomplete.

MediaAnalysisJobs.description: Contains a list of all media analysis jobs.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of stream processors.

StreamProcessors.description: List of stream processors that you have created.

FaceCount.description: The number of faces that are indexed into the collection. To index faces into a collection, use IndexFaces.

FaceModelVersion.description:
The version of the face model that's used by the collection for face detection.

For more information, see Model versioning in the Amazon Rekognition Developer Guide.

CollectionARN.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the collection.

CreationTimestamp.description: The number of milliseconds since the Unix epoch time until the creation of the collection. The Unix epoch time is 00:00:00 Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), Thursday, 1 January 1970.

UserCount.description: The number of UserIDs assigned to the specified colleciton.

ProjectVersionDescriptions.description: A list of project version descriptions. The list is sorted by the creation date and time of the project versions, latest to earliest.

NextToken.description: If the previous response was incomplete (because there is more results to retrieve), Amazon Rekognition returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of results.

StatusMessage.description: Detailed status message about the stream processor.

CreationTimestamp.description: Date and time the stream processor was created

LastUpdateTimestamp.description: The time, in Unix format, the stream processor was last updated. For example, when the stream processor moves from a running state to a failed state, or when the user starts or stops the stream processor.

Input.description: Kinesis video stream that provides the source streaming video.

Output.description: Kinesis data stream to which Amazon Rekognition Video puts the analysis results.

RoleArn.description: ARN of the IAM role that allows access to the stream processor.

Settings.description: Input parameters used in a streaming video analyzed by a stream processor. You can use FaceSearch to recognize faces in a streaming video, or you can use ConnectedHome to detect labels.

NotificationChannel.description:
The Amazon Simple Notification Service topic to which Amazon Rekognition publishes the object detection results and completion status of a video analysis operation.

Amazon Rekognition publishes a notification the first time an object of interest or a person is detected in the video stream. For example, if Amazon Rekognition detects a person at second 2, a pet at second 4, and a person again at second 5, Amazon Rekognition sends 2 object class detected notifications, one for a person at second 2 and one for a pet at second 4.

Amazon Rekognition also publishes an an end-of-session notification with a summary when the stream processing session is complete.

KmsKeyId.description: The identifier for your AWS Key Management Service key (AWS KMS key). This is an optional parameter for label detection stream processors.

RegionsOfInterest.description: Specifies locations in the frames where Amazon Rekognition checks for objects or people. This is an optional parameter for label detection stream processors.

DataSharingPreference.description: Shows whether you are sharing data with Rekognition to improve model performance. You can choose this option at the account level or on a per-stream basis. Note that if you opt out at the account level this setting is ignored on individual streams.

Urls.description: An array of URLs pointing to additional celebrity information.

KnownGender.description: Retrieves the known gender for the celebrity.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the celebrity recognition job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition Video analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from a Amazon Rekognition Video operation.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of celebrities.

Celebrities.description: Array of celebrities recognized in the video.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the celebrity recognition operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartCelebrityRecognition.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartCelebrityRecognition and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the content moderation analysis job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from GetContentModeration.

ModerationLabels.description: The detected inappropriate, unwanted, or offensive content moderation labels and the time(s) they were detected.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of content moderation labels.

ModerationModelVersion.description: Version number of the moderation detection model that was used to detect inappropriate, unwanted, or offensive content.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the content moderation operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartContentModeration.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartContentModeration and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

GetRequestMetadata.description: Information about the paramters used when getting a response. Includes information on aggregation and sorting methods.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the face detection job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition Video analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from a Amazon Rekognition video operation.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of faces.

Faces.description: An array of faces detected in the video. Each element contains a detected face's details and the time, in milliseconds from the start of the video, the face was detected.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the face detection operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartFaceDetection.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartFaceDetection and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

SessionId.description: The sessionId for which this request was called.

Status.description: Represents a status corresponding to the state of the session. Possible statuses are: CREATED, IN_PROGRESS, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, EXPIRED.

Confidence.description: Probabalistic confidence score for if the person in the given video was live, represented as a float value between 0 to 100.

ReferenceImage.description: A high-quality image from the Face Liveness video that can be used for face comparison or search. It includes a bounding box of the face and the Base64-encoded bytes that return an image. If the CreateFaceLivenessSession request included an OutputConfig argument, the image will be uploaded to an S3Object specified in the output configuration. In case the reference image is not returned, it's recommended to retry the Liveness check.

AuditImages.description: A set of images from the Face Liveness video that can be used for audit purposes. It includes a bounding box of the face and the Base64-encoded bytes that return an image. If the CreateFaceLivenessSession request included an OutputConfig argument, the image will be uploaded to an S3Object specified in the output configuration. If no Amazon S3 bucket is defined, raw bytes are sent instead.

Challenge.description: Contains information regarding the challenge type used for the Face Liveness check.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the face search job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of search results.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from a Amazon Rekognition Video operation.

Persons.description: An array of persons, PersonMatch, in the video whose face(s) match the face(s) in an Amazon Rekognition collection. It also includes time information for when persons are matched in the video. You specify the input collection in an initial call to StartFaceSearch. Each Persons element includes a time the person was matched, face match details (FaceMatches) for matching faces in the collection, and person information (Person) for the matched person.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the face search operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartFaceSearch.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartFaceSearch and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the label detection job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition Video analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from a Amazon Rekognition video operation.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of labels.

Labels.description: An array of labels detected in the video. Each element contains the detected label and the time, in milliseconds from the start of the video, that the label was detected.

LabelModelVersion.description: Version number of the label detection model that was used to detect labels.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the label detection operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartLabelDetection.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartLabelDetection and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

GetRequestMetadata.description: Information about the paramters used when getting a response. Includes information on aggregation and sorting methods.

JobId.description: The identifier for the media analysis job.

JobName.description: The name of the media analysis job.

OperationsConfig.description: Operation configurations that were provided during job creation.

Status.description: The current status of the media analysis job.

FailureDetails.description: Details about the error that resulted in failure of the job.

CreationTimestamp.description: The Unix date and time when the job was started.

CompletionTimestamp.description: The Unix date and time when the job finished.

Input.description: Reference to the input manifest that was provided in the job creation request.

OutputConfig.description: Output configuration that was provided in the creation request.

KmsKeyId.description: KMS Key that was provided in the creation request.

ManifestSummary.description: The summary manifest provides statistics on input manifest and errors identified in the input manifest.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the person tracking job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition Video analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from a Amazon Rekognition Video operation.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of persons.

Persons.description: An array of the persons detected in the video and the time(s) their path was tracked throughout the video. An array element will exist for each time a person's path is tracked.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the person tracking operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartPersonTracking.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartCelebrityRecognition and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

JobStatus.description: Current status of the segment detection job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Currently, Amazon Rekognition Video returns a single object in the VideoMetadata array. The object contains information about the video stream in the input file that Amazon Rekognition Video chose to analyze. The VideoMetadata object includes the video codec, video format and other information. Video metadata is returned in each page of information returned by GetSegmentDetection.

AudioMetadata.description: An array of objects. There can be multiple audio streams. Each AudioMetadata object contains metadata for a single audio stream. Audio information in an AudioMetadata objects includes the audio codec, the number of audio channels, the duration of the audio stream, and the sample rate. Audio metadata is returned in each page of information returned by GetSegmentDetection.

NextToken.description: If the previous response was incomplete (because there are more labels to retrieve), Amazon Rekognition Video returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of text.

Segments.description: An array of segments detected in a video. The array is sorted by the segment types (TECHNICAL_CUE or SHOT) specified in the SegmentTypes input parameter of StartSegmentDetection. Within each segment type the array is sorted by timestamp values.

SelectedSegmentTypes.description: An array containing the segment types requested in the call to StartSegmentDetection.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the segment detection operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartSegmentDetection.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartSegmentDetection and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

JobStatus.description: Current status of the text detection job.

StatusMessage.description: If the job fails, StatusMessage provides a descriptive error message.

VideoMetadata.description: Information about a video that Amazon Rekognition analyzed. Videometadata is returned in every page of paginated responses from a Amazon Rekognition video operation.

TextDetections.description: An array of text detected in the video. Each element contains the detected text, the time in milliseconds from the start of the video that the text was detected, and where it was detected on the screen.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition Video returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of text.

TextModelVersion.description: Version number of the text detection model that was used to detect text.

JobId.description: Job identifier for the text detection operation for which you want to obtain results. The job identifer is returned by an initial call to StartTextDetection.

Video.description: Video file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Amazon Rekognition video start operations such as StartLabelDetection use Video to specify a video for analysis. The supported file formats are .mp4, .mov and .avi.

JobTag.description: A job identifier specified in the call to StartTextDetection and returned in the job completion notification sent to your Amazon Simple Notification Service topic.

DatasetEntries.description: A list of entries (images) in the dataset.

NextToken.description: If the previous response was incomplete (because there is more results to retrieve), Amazon Rekognition Custom Labels returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of results.

DatasetLabelDescriptions.description: A list of the labels in the dataset.

NextToken.description: If the previous response was incomplete (because there is more results to retrieve), Amazon Rekognition Custom Labels returns a pagination token in the response. You can use this pagination token to retrieve the next set of results.
If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of faces.

FaceModelVersion.description: Version number of the face detection model associated with the input collection (CollectionId).

ProjectPolicies.description: A list of project policies attached to the project.

NextToken.description: If the response is truncated, Amazon Rekognition returns this token that you can use in the subsequent request to retrieve the next set of project policies.

Tags.description: A list of key-value tags assigned to the resource.

Users.description: List of UsersID associated with the specified collection.

NextToken.description: A pagination token to be used with the subsequent request if the response is truncated.

SearchedFaceId.description: ID of the face that was searched for matches in a collection.

FaceMatches.description: An array of faces that matched the input face, along with the confidence in the match.

FaceModelVersion.description: Version number of the face detection model associated with the input collection (CollectionId).

SearchedFaceBoundingBox.description: The bounding box around the face in the input image that Amazon Rekognition used for the search.

SearchedFaceConfidence.description: The level of confidence that the searchedFaceBoundingBox, contains a face.

FaceMatches.description: An array of faces that match the input face, along with the confidence in the match.

FaceModelVersion.description: Version number of the face detection model associated with the input collection (CollectionId).

UserMatches.description: An array of UserMatch objects that matched the input face along with the confidence in the match. Array will be empty if there are no matches.

FaceModelVersion.description: Version number of the face detection model associated with the input CollectionId.

SearchedFace.description: Contains the ID of a face that was used to search for matches in a collection.

SearchedUser.description: Contains the ID of the UserID that was used to search for matches in a collection.

UserMatches.description: An array of UserID objects that matched the input face, along with the confidence in the match. The returned structure will be empty if there are no matches. Returned if the SearchUsersByImageResponse action is successful.

FaceModelVersion.description: Version number of the face detection model associated with the input collection CollectionId.

SearchedFace.description: A list of FaceDetail objects containing the BoundingBox for the largest face in image, as well as the confidence in the bounding box, that was searched for matches. If no valid face is detected in the image the response will contain no SearchedFace object.

UnsearchedFaces.description: List of UnsearchedFace objects. Contains the face details infered from the specified image but not used for search. Contains reasons that describe why a face wasn't used for Search.
